description: Level-b monitor (research pipeline step 3): find recent literature beyond the shelf, triage cheaply for whether each paper changes the guideline vs what the synthesis ALREADY says, then a (stronger) model proposes deltas — additions/modifications beyond current guidance. Empty is a valid answer. Budget-disciplined: bounded candidate set, cheap Gemma triage, strong model only on the short list. Deterministic checks live in tests/scripts, not here.

nodes.label: Start
nodes.description: Entry. Receives disease_slug + disease_name; requires an existing synthesis (level a).

nodes.label: Load guidance + recent papers
nodes.description: Load the current synthesis (what we already say) + shelf PMIDs, then fetch recent papers beyond the shelf (bounded).

nodes.label: Triage (cheap): does it change anything?
nodes.description: Cheap pass (Gemma): score each recent paper on the chance it adds/changes something NOT already in the current guidance.

nodes.label: Propose deltas (stronger model)
nodes.description: From the high-probability papers, propose additions/modifications beyond current guidance. Set model_name to a stronger model here for the final judgement; empty is valid.

nodes.label: Write suggestions
nodes.description: Map deltas onto guideline_suggestions and replace (terminal). gate=expert; promotion to parent is a separate D5 signal.
